title: acer tenuifolium
intro: Acer tenuifolium, also known as the Red Alder, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its active compounds, including salicin and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Red Alder include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
